title: ampelocissus javalensis
intro: Ampelocissus javalensis, also known as Javal, is a type of vine native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It is a member of the grape family (Vitaceae) and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ampelocissus javalensis include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used to make a paste or a tincture for topical application.
